title: rubus microphyllus var. subcrataegifolius
intro: Rubus microphyllus var. subcrataegifolius, commonly known as the Japanese raspberry or thimbleberry, is a variety of the thimbleberry plant, which is a type of bramble native to East Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. The health benefits of Rubus microphyllus var. subcrataegifolius include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being, and it can be used to prepare teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal purposes. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin.
